steps.title: Separate two main clauses
steps.content: The sentence contains two independent clauses: the museum has celebrated contemporary Native artists since 1989, and in 2015 it awarded Wilson a fellowship. A semicolon can join those closely related clauses.

steps.title: Choose the clean boundary
steps.content: A blurs the two time frames with and. C creates a comma splice between independent clauses. D adds thus even though the second clause is a specific example, not a stated consequence. The semicolon gives the needed clause boundary. The answer is B.
